column2: LISA 會將三艘太空船放喺環繞太陽嘅軌道上，跟住地球後面飛。三艘太空船之間相隔500萬公里，組成一個三角形陣式飛行。當引力波經過嗰陣，呢啲太空嘅漣漪會極輕微咁推移太空船，幅度細到難以想像。但三艘太空船之間連接嘅超級－超級－超敏感激光束，就能測量到呢啲極微小嘅移動。
column4: LISA will place three spacecraft in orbit around the Sun, tagging along behind Earth. The three spacecraft will be 5 million kilometers apart flying in a triangle formation. When a gravitational wave passes by, these ripples in space gently push the spacecraft around by the tiniest amount imaginable. But the super-super-super sensitive laser beams that connect the three spacecraft will allow this tiny movement to be measured.

column2: 老實講，我真係受唔住「empathy」呢個字。我覺得「empathy」係一個人為製造出嚟嘅新時代術語，帶嚟好多壞處。我好鍾意用「compassion」同「sympathy」呢兩個字。Empathy 係試圖將人哋嘅痛苦同悲傷當成自己嘅嚟感受；compassion 就容許理解。我更偏好「sympathy」，因為政治已經將 empathy 武器化咗。
column4: I can’t stand the word 'empathy', actually. I think 'empathy' is a made-up, new-age term, and it does a lot of damage. I much prefer the word 'compassion', and I much prefer the word 'sympathy'. Empathy is where you try to feel someone’s pain and sorrows as if they’re your own. Compassion allows for understanding. I prefer the word 'sympathy', because politics has weaponized empathy.

column2: 大部分歐洲語言入面「Finns」（芬蘭人）呢個字，源自古代日耳曼民族畀佢哋嘅名稱，呢個名稱大概意思係「覓食者」（finders of food）或者「流浪者」（wanderers），反映喺嗰個遙遠時代，芬蘭人仍然係獵人採集者，冇固定居所，而日耳曼部落已經轉向定居農業。芬蘭人自己對國家嘅名稱「Suomi」，同佢哋嘅鄰居薩米人（Sami）嘅名稱有關；但佢嘅語源至今仍然未有定論。
column4: The word for Finns in most European languages derives from the name given to them by ancient Germanic peoples, which probably meant 'finders (of food)' or 'wanderers', reflecting that, in those distant times, Finns were still hunter-gatherers lacking a fixed abode, while Germanic tribes had already switched to sedentary agriculture. The Finnish name for their country, 'Suomi', is related to the name of their Sami neighbours; its etymology remains unclear.

column2: 喺所有暴政當中，最壓迫人嘅，可能就係嗰種出於真心為受害者好而行使嘅暴政。寧願生活喺強盜貴族之下，都好過生活喺無所不能、道德多管閒事嘅人手底下。強盜貴族嘅殘忍有時會停一停，佢嘅貪婪有時會滿足；但嗰啲為咗我哋「好」而折磨我哋嘅人，會無止境咁折磨落去，因為佢哋做嘅時候，有自己良心嘅認可。
column4: Of all tyrannies, a tyranny sincerely exercised for the good of its victims may be the most oppressive. It would be better to live under robber barons than under omnipotent moral busybodies. The robber baron's cruelty may sometimes sleep, his cupidity may at some point be satiated; but those who torment us for our own good will torment us without end, for they do so with the approval of their own conscience.

column2: 「我有一陣時有條路入黑手黨。」「哦係咩？」「係呀，有個叫 Anto 嘅人——」「唔使講佢名。我唔需要知佢名。你點解要講佢名畀我聽？」「我退伍嗰陣，佢話如果我想做，佢有份工畀我。」「你冇接？」「冇，聽落壓力太大喇。」「我大概都會做同樣選擇。至少我希望自己會咁做。」
column4: I had an in to the Mafia at one point. "Oh yeah?" "Yeah, this guy Anto—" "I don't need to know his name. Why are you telling me his name?" "He told me when I got out of the service that there was a job for me if I wanted one." "You didn't take it?" "No, that sounded like way too much stress." "I probably would have done the same. I hope, anyway."

column2: 我好清楚發脾氣係啲乜嘢事。你見過我失控個樣。但就算喺我最差嘅時候，我都唔會故意殺人！就算真係殺咗，我知道自己一定會好深切咁後悔。即使嗰一刻覺得自己有理、出氣，但冷靜落嚟之後，我一定會為自己嘅行為感到悔恨。我絕對唔會對奪取一條人命咁輕浮、咁冇所謂，你都唔應該咁！
column4: I know what it means to have a temper. You’ve seen what I'm like when I lose control. But even at my worst, I'd never willingly kill someone! And if I did, I know I'd feel some serious remorse for it. Even if I'd felt vindicated in the moment, once I calmed down, I'd regret my actions. I would never be so blasé or flippant about taking a human life, and neither should you!

column2: 呢個唔係精英主義嘅問題；呢個係生活質素嘅問題。你想同人講，佢哋關心乾淨空氣同乾淨水嘅憂慮同渴望係「精英主義」？你去同南布朗克斯（South Bronx）嘅細路講啦，佢哋係全國童年哮喘率最高嘅地方。你去同弗林特（Flint）嘅家庭講返，佢哋嘅細路血液含鉛量上升，腦部永久受損，一世都修復唔到。你去叫佢哋「精英」試吓。
column4: This is not an elitist issue; this is a quality-of-life issue. You want to tell people that their concern and their desire for clean air and clean water is elitist? Tell that to the kids in the South Bronx, which are suffering from the highest rates of childhood asthma in the country. Tell that to the families in Flint, whose kids have—their blood is ascending in lead levels. Their brains are damaged for the rest of their lives. Call them elitist.

column2: 唔係霧本身令我介意，Cathleen。我真係好鍾意霧。霧會將你同世界隔開，將世界同你隔開。你會覺得一切都變咗樣，冇一樣嘢仲似之前嘅模樣。冇人再搵到你，觸摸到你。我討厭嘅係嗰個霧號（foghorn）。佢唔肯放過你，成日喺度提醒你、警告你、叫你返轉頭。
column4: It wasn't the fog I minded, Cathleen. I really love fog. It hides you from the world and the world from you. You feel that everything has changed, and nothing is what it seemed to be. No one can find or touch you any more. It's the foghorn I hate. It won't let you alone. It keeps reminding you, and warning you, and calling you back.

column2: 「倘若有一日我見到台伯河同佢兩岸嘅田野，畀佢嘅浪花沖洗，仲未混入鹹水之前；倘若我按命運所定，建立起呢座城邦；咁樣，親族之城同鄰近民族將會聯合，你嘅喺伊庇魯斯，我嘅喺赫斯珀里亞；從此之後，兩地人民喺悲傷同歡樂中連繫起來，以達達努斯作為兩脈嘅共同始祖；願後世子孫致力於此，兩個民族同心一德，再造過另一個特洛伊。」
column4: If ever Tiber and the fields I see / washed by her waves, ere mingling with the brine, / and build the city which the Fates decree, / then kindred towns and neighbouring folk shall join, / yours in Epirus, in Hesperia mine, / and linked thenceforth in sorrow and in joy, / with Dardanus the founder of each line, / so let posterity its pains employ, / two nations, one in heart, shall make another Troy.

column2: 呢單案入面，有一樣嘢令僕人同警方都印象最深刻，就係上校嘅面容扭曲。據佢哋描述，佢嘅臉定型咗，變成人類面容所能表現出最可怕嘅恐懼同驚駭表情。好多人單單望到佢，就已經暈倒，因為嗰種恐怖嘅效果實在太駭人。
column4: There was one thing in the case which had made the deepest impression both upon the servants and the police. This was the contortion of the Colonel's face. It had set, according to their account, into the most dreadful expression of fear and horror which a human countenance is capable of assuming. More than one person fainted at the mere sight of him, so terrible was the effect.

column2: 政治正確有兩種經常被混淆，但應該分開嚟講嘅類型。一種係語言上嘅政治正確，認為某些群體唔應該被用帶負面意味嘅字眼稱呼（例如種族侮辱詞）；另一種係理念上嘅政治正確，認為某些想法唔應該被討論。前者可以話係值得讚許嘅；後者則有害。
column4: There are two types of political correctness which are often conflated and which should be distinguished. There is the political correctness of language, which holds that certain groups should not be referred to by certain words with negative connotations (e.g., ethnic slurs), and there is the political correctness of ideas, which holds that certain ideas should not be discussed. The former is arguably commendable; the latter is harmful.

column2: 就好似初夏嘅蜜蜂成群飛出，穿梭喺花田之間，從山谷同溪澗帶出成年嘅後代；或者將液態蜂蜜儲存喺每一個蜂巢格，令滿溢嘅蜂巢充滿甜美花蜜。有啲蜜蜂幫新來者卸下啲負荷，有啲將冇用嘅雄蜂趕走。每隻蜜蜂都忙住自己嘅工作，蜂巢充滿百里香同蜂蜜嘅甜香。
column4: As bees in early summer swarm apace / through flowery fields, when forth from dale and dell / they lead the full-grown offspring of the race, / or with the liquid honey store each cell, / and make the teeming hive with nectarous sweets to swell. / These ease the comers of their loads, those drive / the drones afar. The busy work each plies, / and sweet with thyme and honey smells the hive.

column2: 「戰爭將會終止，野蠻時代變得溫和，雷穆斯同奎里努斯，呢對兄弟，連同埋白髮蒼蒼嘅信義同純潔無瑕嘅維斯塔，將會頒布法則。雅努斯神廟嘅大門，會用鐵閂同鎖鏈牢牢關閉。廟內，紛爭惡魔高踞喺可怕嘅兵器之上，冇人理會，喺神殿深處畀一百條銅鏈從後綁住，血盆大口噉猙獰，磨緊佢可怕嘅牙齒。」
column4: Then wars shall cease and savage times grow mild, / and Remus and Quirinus, brethren twain, / with hoary Faith and Vesta undefiled, / shall give the law. With iron bolt and chain / firm-closed the gates of Janus shall remain. / Within, the Fiend of Discord, high reclined / on horrid arms, unheeded in the fane, / bound with a hundred brazen knots behind, / and grim with gory jaws, his grisly teeth shall grind.

column2: 「Mary，歷史話羅馬帝國用麵包同馬戲團嚟滿足大眾，但其實係羅馬寡頭集團一個巨大陰謀，目的係改變拉丁文本身嘅結構。佢哋用一種叫『代名詞解耦』嘅方法，將拉丁文內在機制拆散，令講者之間幾乎完全聽唔明白對方。所以我琴晚先至飲醉咗，」Tom 講。
column4: Mary, history says that the Roman Empire had bread and circuses for the masses, but really it was a vast conspiracy by the Roman oligarchy to change the very structure of Latin itself. By using a method of 'pronounic decoupling', the implicit mechanisms of Latin were disengaged and its speakers were hardly ever able to understand each other. And that's why I got drunk last night, said Tom.

column2: 呢啲命令其實唔需要，因為一刻鐘之後，公園周圍就長出極大量嘅樹木，有大有細，仲有灌木同荊棘，互相纏繞交織，無論人定獸都穿唔過；結果除咗從遠處勉強見到宮殿塔樓嘅頂端之外，乜都睇唔到。
column4: These orders were not necessary; for in a quarter of an hour's time there grew up all round about the park such a vast number of trees, great and small, bushes and brambles, twining one within another, that neither man nor beast could pass through; so that nothing could be seen but the very top of the towers of the palace; and that, too, only from afar off.

column2: 愛情：一種暫時性精神錯亂，可透過結婚或將患者移離引發病症嘅環境而治癒。呢種病，好似蛀牙同好多其他病症一樣，只喺文明種族、活喺人為環境之下先至流行；野蠻民族呼吸純淨空氣、食簡單食物，就完全免疫。有時會致命，但通常致命嘅對象係醫生多過病人。
column4: Love: A temporary insanity curable by marriage or by the removal of the patient from the influences under which he incurred the disorder. This disease, like caries and many other ailments, is prevalent only among civilized races living under artificial conditions; barbarous nations breathing pure air and eating simple food enjoy immunity from its ravages. It is sometimes fatal, but more frequently to the physician than to the patient.

column2: 有時喺我自己國家，我因為相信國際規範同多邊機構而被批評。但我堅信，長遠嚟講，放棄一部分行動自由——唔係放棄保護自己或追求核心利益嘅能力，而係長期將自己綁喺國際規則之上——其實會增強我哋嘅安全。我覺得呢個唔單止適用於我哋，對其他國家都一樣。
column4: Sometimes I'm criticized in my own country for professing a belief in international norms and multilateral institutions. But I am convinced that in the long run, giving up some freedom of action -- not giving up our ability to protect ourselves or pursue our core interests, but binding ourselves to international rules over the long term -- enhances our security. And I think that's not just true for us.

column2: 有時我個身體真係好攰，好想由呢陣無止境嘅奔波入面停低抖吓。但轉頭一諗起我阿公阿嫲（祖父母），當年佢哋嚟到呢度嗰陣一無所有，卻為咗呢個國家傾盡所有，我嗰陣攰意即刻就消散晒。因為我對佢哋、對埋下一代，都有一份道義上嘅債——我要繼續為咗一個繁榮同自由嘅委內瑞拉而奮鬥。喺嗰度，付出係應該有回報嘅，而政治應該係一種服務而唔係一盤生意。唯有咁樣，我哋先至唔使再喺機場同任何人道別，唔使再感受嗰種心碎到成地碎片嘅痛。
column4: Sometimes my body asks for a break from so much running around, but then I think of my grandparents who arrived here with nothing and gave everything for this country, and the tiredness disappears at once, because I have a moral debt to them and to those who come after them to continue fighting for a prosperous and free Venezuela, where effort is rewarded and where politics is a service and not a business, so that we never again have to say goodbye to anyone at an airport with our hearts broken into a thousand pieces.

column2: 有個共產黨員死咗，因為佢份人雖然係無神論者，但生前都算老實，所以最後被判「一年住地獄，一年住天堂」咁樣輪流轉。一年過去，撒旦頂唔順走去同上帝講：「你快啲帶走呢個人啦！佢將我下面啲後生魔鬼全部變晒做『少先隊』，搞到我而家要大費周章去重整紀律呀！」又一年過去，撒旦又返嚟提醒上帝：「上帝大佬，而家輪到我接手嗰個人喇。」點知上帝回頭望一望佢，冷靜啲噉講：「第一，唔好嗌我做『上帝大佬』，要嗌『上帝同志』；第二，呢個世界根本就冇上帝；第三，你唔好喺度騷擾我，如果唔係我趕唔切去參加『黨支部會議』呀！」
column4: A communist dies, and since he was an honest man, albeit atheistic, he is sentenced to rotate spending one year in Hell and one year in Heaven. One year passes, and Satan tells God, "Take this man as fast as possible, because he turned all my young demons into Young Pioneers; I have to restore some order." Another year passes. "Lord God, it's my turn now," Satan reminds. God replies, "First of all, don't call me Lord God, but instead Comrade God; second, there is no God; and one more thing – don't distract me, or I'll be late to the Party meeting."

column2: 有個緝毒局（DEA）嘅探員喺德州一個牧場停低。佢好大口氣噉同個牧場主人講：「我要搜查你個牧場，睇吓你有冇非法種植毒品。」老牧場主人答佢：「冇問題，但你千祈唔好去嗰邊嗰塊草地呀。」佢指住其中一個方向。個探員一聽即刻「爆氣」：「先生，我代表緊聯邦政府嘅權威！」佢由後褲袋攞個委任證出嚟，好神氣噉喺個老牧場主人面前晃兩晃：「見唔見到個證呀？呢個證代表我可以去任何我想去嘅地方。任何土地。唔使問問題，亦都唔使向人交代。我講得夠唔夠清楚？你明唔明呀？」老牧場主人好有禮貌噉點點頭，道咗個歉，就繼續去做佢啲農活。過咗冇耐，老牧場主人聽到一陣好淒厲嘅尖叫聲，一抬頭就見到個探員搏命跑緊，後面有一隻超級憤怒嘅大公牛追住佢，想攞佢命噉款。每跑一步，隻公牛就拉近一點距離，眼見個探員未跑到安全地方，就肯定會畀牛角插穿。個探員嚇到魂飛魄散。個老牧場主人即刻揼低手上啲架生，跑去圍欄邊用盡全身力氣大叫：「個證呀！攞你個死人委任證畀佢睇呀！！」
column4: A DEA officer stops at a ranch in Texas. He tells the rancher, "I need to inspect your ranch for illegally grown drugs." The rancher says, "Okay, but do not go in that field over there," as he points out the location. The DEA officer verbally explodes, "Mister, I have the authority of the Federal Government with me." Reaching into his rear pants pocket, he takes out his badge and proudly displays it to the rancher. "See this badge? This badge means I am allowed to go wherever I wish. On any land. No questions asked or answers given. Have I made myself clear? Do you understand?" The rancher nods politely, apologizes, and goes about his chores. A short while later, the old rancher hears loud screams and sees the DEA officer running for his life, chased by the rancher's big angry bull... With every step, the bull is gaining ground on the officer, and it seems likely that he'll get gored before he reaches safety. The officer is clearly terrified. The rancher throws down his tools, runs to the fence and yells at the top of his lungs, "Your badge! Show him your fucking badge!"
